candidate_output: The core of “The Next Best Principle” is to “lower the threshold” - what it encourages is a kind of “small wins makes for big wins” theory, that is, if you cannot do 100, just do 1: because 1 is always greater than 0; if you don’t get to Mount Tai, you can just climb the small sand dunes at your doorstep, because that is always better than standing still.
dimension_question_en: Is the candidate translation accurate, fluent, and adequate with respect to the source and reference?
reference: The core of the “second best principle” is lowering the threshold; it encourages “accumulating small successes to have big success”; even if you cannot reach 100, you can be 1, because 1 is always bigger than 0; if you cannot climb on Mount Tai, you can climb on the small dune in front of your house, because that is still better than marching on the spot.
source_text: “次好原则”的核心在于“降低门槛 ” — —它鼓励的是一种“积小胜为大胜”的理论，即如果做不到100，只做到1也行：因为1永远大于0；如果登不上泰山，只登上你家门口的小沙丘也行，因为那也总比原地踏步强。
